examples: At 25, she found family life stifling.
There was a stifling smell of hot oil everywhere.
It has been said of Beethoven that he toppled the rules and freed music from the stifling confines of convention.
During one of her chemo regimes, Barry was in a board meeting in a stifling hot room.
The IRS has usually done an excellent job of stifling investigations of its practices.
